title: ExtraDelight
description: Extra Delight is an addon for Farmer's Delight with the aim to bring more cooking workstations and recipes to go along with them.
body:
Extra Delight is a mod that vigorously expands upon farming and cooking in Farmer's Delight.

  Oven - A 3x3 crafting grid that slowly bakes recipes as long as there is a heat source beneath and the appropriate bakeware in the tray slot. Dough Shaping Station - A stone cutter for dough! Shape pasta or cookies from a variety of doughs. Mixing Bowl - Mix up all sorts of tasty treats! Load in the appropriate items (and perhaps fluids) and right-click on it with the appropriate utensil. Mortar - Used to crush ingredients to get at the goodness inside - your first step towards bread made without straw! Will require its trusty partner the Pestle. Can also give fluids like Cooking Oil. Drying Rack - Gotta get dry! Make snacks like jerky or dried fruit, or dry ingredients so they can be ground into powder. Melting Pot - Melt solids into liquids. Requires a heat source underneath. Chiller - Chill liquids into solids. Can create a variety of (mainly chocolatey) treats. Requires some sort of container for the fluids to set in. And having the drip tray emptied occasionally. Spoon - You spin me right round, baby. Mix things up in the Mixing Bowl, scoop up tasty treats, or even peel ginger on the FD Cutting Board! Pestle - The grind don’t stop, and neither should you. Crush everything from wheat seeds, to roasted coffee beans, to dried agar agar sheets, or even flowers to get a bit more dye out of them. In the Mortar of course. Bakeware/trays - For use in the oven (and the chiller occasionally too). A baking sheet, a muffin pan, a pie dish and more! Grater - Totally shredded. Grate cheese, potatoes, carrots, and more on the FD Cutting Board   Offset spatula - Your kitchen’s fairy godmother! A wave of this magic wand and your kitchen is re-styled. Works on frosted gingerbread and chocolate blocks to change the patterns, as well as giving your Mortars, Mixing Bowls, and bakeware a new look. Funnel - A hopper for fluids! Can drain in to the Mixing Bowl, out of the Mortar, and in and out of a Keg, Jar, or Chiller. Or you can manually tip fluid in the top using a bucket or Jar. It will even pick up a fluid block (if one happens to be sitting on top of it) and spit it out the bottom if there is space. Jar - A means of picking up less than (and up to) a bucket of a fluid. For example, it can be used to scoop fluid out of the Mortar and transfer it to the Mixing Bowl, or just place it down on a shelf. Keg - Stores up to 8 buckets of one fluid. Best friends with the Funnel. Tap - In its raw form. Right-click with a bucket to fill the bucket with water. Drippin’.   All Extra Delight crops use biome tags, so will be able to be found in all modded biomes as well as Vanilla ones. Bees can pollinate them, and some of them can also be used to breed animals. Cinnamon - Trees with tasty, tasty bark that grow in jungle biomes. Ginger - A zesty root with a pretty red flower found in hot biomes. Mint - The mint must spread! Found in cold biomes. It's much happier in warm or temperate biomes though. Corn - A big ol’ stalk of grass found in the drier types of plains biomes. Mallow - Great swathes of white flowers found in swampy biomes. Chili - Hot, hot, hot! Found in hot biomes of course. Peanut - Is it a pea? Is it a nut? Well whatever it is, you’ll find its little yellow flowers in jungle biomes. Hazelnut - Nuts for Hazelnuts! Yes, you can make a certain chocolate-y spread out of it… Look for it in forest biomes. Coffee - Is there too much blood in your caffeine system? Then you’ll want to go hunting in jungle biomes for these little bushes with their red and yellow cherries to slake your cravings. Apple - Yes, now you can keep the doctor away without deforesting the neighbourhood! Which is appropriate seeing as it spawns in forest biomes. Garlic - Onion's little brother joins the family! Find it on temperate mountain slopes.   Need some Vinegar in your life? How about some Breadcrumbs? Maybe some Frosting, or Cookie Dough? Condiments like Mayo or Ketchup? Cornmeal? Butter? Gravy? Whipped Cream?! Suffice to say there’s a lot of things to work with in Extra Delight (over a hundred!), and even more things to make them into! And for those of you who need every fluid to come in a bucket we offer you 31 options, some of which you miiiight not want to step in…
   ____ 115 savory Meals Fried foods, salads, pasta, burgers, breakfast cereals. 92 sweet, sweet Desserts Custards, ice creams, baked goods, popsicles! 64 Candies for after All the chocolate you could ever want, mints, caramel. 23 Drinks to quench the deepest of thirsts Juices, milkshakes, hot chocolate three ways, even eggnog. 22 Cookies for all you monsters out there Shaped holiday cookies galore. 17 Snacks for when you just gotta munch on something. Chips, popcorn, jerky Another 28 more meaty delights, if you have Butchercraft.   ____ A mighty 78 (assuming you have Butchercraft) feasts can grace your banquet table. All so nice to look at, and most with guilty traces left behind… Tuck into a hearty lasagna, dish yourself up some chili, slice up one of seven cheesecakes, dip any number of things into a chocolate fondue, or even sink into a whole rainbow of jelly.   ____ Two new wood sets! Cinnamon wood is a lighter, brighter orange than Acacia, while Fruit wood is a lighter, brighter sibling to Jungle. It’s even got the horizontal bark pattern. ![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXecGYV2nEQasTsH1-TMmXltH9qHd-t_V5tA1weYx225dsGoP145ellNGp-Gr-hYf4iGIu6U0aEKoiC6XxNAGh_Q3Zl1Qu5stw8uXMrypPnuvgPlOKXBOKbACkjV7BvWcky__OHYRA?key=qrDTHmrylnAmnfWStjqRVQ) All wooden items can be crafted in all Vanilla and Extra Delight wood types. Wooden Stuff Wallpaper - Comes in all 16 colours, and can have a wooden molding which can then be placed at the top or bottom of the block. Spice Racks - Cute little shelves which have 4 inventory slots. They can be crafted in their “filled” variant to display some decorative jars, or “unfilled” to display the items stored. Half Cabinets - Like the FD cabinets but only half as deep! Great for using as overhead cabinets, and can sneakily store just as much as their full-size siblings… Knife Blocks - Visually store up to 4 knives (including Butchercraft ones!) Step Stools - A handy step up. Acts like a slab in that you will automatically step up onto it. Countertop Cabinets - Look just like their regular FD cousins… until you click the cogwheel and give it any block with a solid texture (no leaves) to use as a countertop texture, upon which it will magically sprout a little counter edge as well. Works with Ice, Glass, slabs, the ED Keg, the FD Stove… The possibilities are endless! Sinks - A cabinet with a sink in it! For all your watery needs. Although it does have reduced storage capacity to make room for the plumbing… But it also has the option to set a countertop texture so it can fit right in with the rest of your benchtop. Dried Corn Fences - Regular Fences not feel rustic enough for you? Try the Dried Corn variant to sprinkle in a little more visual interest. Non-Wooden Stuff Wreaths - Well they’re wood-adjacent? Make a festive wreath out of any leaf type and then display 8… anythings on it. Bows - The Wreaths’ best friends! Come in all 16 wool colours and can be put on Wreaths, worn in your hair, or crafted into a pretty box… Chocolate Boxes - They’re boxes. You put chocolate (or candy) in them. And then close the lid, pick them up, and give them to someone special Candy Bowl - Very similar in use, but only available in one colour and cannot be picked up while keeping the contents. Sure looks pretty on your counter though! Food Display - And if there’s something you want to display without it being jumbled up in a bowl, look no further than the Food Display! Feel like opening a supermarket? You can even add a glass frontage by right-clicking on it with a glass block. Three (or maybe four) sets of chocolate building blocks. They’re not quite full “sets”, but have blocks, stairs, slabs, doors, trapdoors, pillars, fences, and fence gates. Go build that castle. Cheese and Butter blocks! Blocks, slabs, and stairs. Storage blocks - Crates and sacks for all the Extra Delight crops (and a few Vanilla things FD missed, like berries and mushrooms), and then sacks and bundles of some of the raw items like Cinnamon Bark, Corn Husks, and Flour. Stack your store rooms high! Corn Husk Doll - A creepy lil doll made out of corn. Hang it from the ceiling if you really want. Corn Cob Pipe - Bubbles! Hanging foods - Ever wanted to hang your pantry from your ceiling? Well Extra Delight makes it possible! Currently available in onion, mint, ham, corn, garlic, and two types of chili. ![](https://media.forgecdn.net/attachments/1138/919/untitled-png.png) Gingham block and carpets - Cute lil checkers to get that cozy kitchen feeling going. Available in all 16 colours (yes, even white).   ____ How do I make Vinegar (or Yeast)?

Have you tried searching for the term “Vinegar” in JEI? Or looking at the Advancements…?

Why doesn’t Coffee Cake have any coffee in it?

Similar reason to why teacakes don’t have any tea in them. It’s for eating with coffee.

What’s with all these meaty/bloody recipes I can’t make?

They require one of ED’s sister mods - Butchercraft!. Or any other meat mod that uses the same tags.

Why can't I make (insert recipe name here)?

Have you checked all the ingredients? If you are using Extra Delight in a modpack it is entirely possible you are using something like butter or flour from a different mod. And while Extra Delight uses tags in its recipes wherever possible… not all mods add their ingredients to the appropriate tags. If you are using something like JEI it should show you which versions of ingredients are able to be used. Otherwise try making the recipe with 100% Extra Delight ingredients, and if you’re still having trouble hop into the Discord and we’ll do our best to get you sorted!

Why can’t I remove items from the Drying Rack or Mortar while holding a shield?

This is just a vanilla thing with shift + right-click unfortunately. You’ll notice if you have a shield on you can’t open a door while crouching either.

Why can’t I make Liquid Blood Chocolate? (Don’t have Butchercraft)

Unfortunately the fluid ingredients are not as obvious when they aren’t available. If you mouse over the recipe you should see a fluid above the Cocoa Butter. This is where Blood would be if you had Butchercraft installed.

Why can't I find the recipe for the other chocolate fluids either?

Awkwardly the chocolate fluids are made in bottles but then used as fluids, and there's currently nothing connecting them. This will be fixed in a future update, but for now try looking up (for example) "Liquid Milk Chocolate" and then select the bottle item.

How do I get into the Corn Dimension?

Never heard of it.

  J.E.I.

The Salt
Tough As Nails
Vampirism   If you have an enchanted/damaged skillet and use it to make Cornbread or the Tarte Tatin you will just get a regular/undamaged one back. Cinnamon/fruit sets are missing signs/hanging signs and boats/chest boats. Fluids destroy each other if they touch. Blood is really not obvious in the Liquid Blood Chocolate recipe when Butchercraft is not installed. No code currently connecting fluids and bottle items.   zh_cn - Thanks Cloud_Meow! fr_fr - Thanks Léon! ru_ru - Thanks mpustovoi!

Older versions will not have all of them.   # Sister mods - [](https://modrinth.com/mod/butchercraft) Get more from your animals.
